research_track: Service Integration & Wiring — fix dead wiring in external_validator + sector crash model
what_i_noticed: Three call sites (market.py:207, stock.py:205, stock.py:365) passed wrong arguments to validate_external() — (data, fred_data) instead of (fred_data, crash_prob, regime) — and called .get() on a dataclass return. Sector router (sector.py:63) called predictor.predict() which doesn't exist on CrashPredictor. All 4 bugs were hidden inside 'except Exception: pass' blocks, so they silently produced None for every request. The external consensus signal (13% weight in signal engine) and sector crash probability were permanently dead.
hypothesis: Fixing the 4 broken call sites will activate the external consensus signal in buy/sell signals and crash-aware sector analysis. Narrowing broad excepts will surface real bugs instead of hiding them. Moving hardcoded thresholds to config.py will make the validator tunable without code changes.
what_i_did: Phase B: Fixed validate_external() calls in 3 routers — corrected argument order (fred_data, crash_12m/100, regime) and attribute access (.consensus_direction instead of .get()). Rewrote sector.py crash model block to use proper load/predict_proba pattern matching other routers. Phase C: Moved 8 hardcoded magic numbers from external_validator.py to config.py (13 named parameters). Narrowed 9 broad 'except Exception: pass' blocks to specific exception tuples with logger.debug(). Removed unused imports (datetime, numpy). Wrote 53 tests covering every _assess_* function, consensus logic, edge cases, and config integration. Phase D: Self-critique found 1 missed broad except in stock.py _analyze_stock — fixed it.
analysis: The fix is real but unverifiable end-to-end without a live server test. The 4 bugs were definitively broken (wrong arg count, wrong method name, .get() on dataclass) so the fixes are certainly correct. However, I cannot confirm the signal engine actually USES the external_consensus parameter effectively — if signal_engine.get_market_signal() ignores or underweights external_consensus=None vs a real value, the fix changes nothing visible. The sector crash_prob similarly needs a live run to see if sector returns actually shift. The hardening (narrow excepts, config extraction, 53 tests) is solid defensive work but doesn't prove the features work end-to-end. The remaining 2 broad excepts I left alone are in _compute_market_status (crash model, data quality, net liquidity blocks) — these are in code I didn't touch and deal with multiple service calls where the exception surface is broader.
what_i_would_do_differently: 1) Should have verified signal_engine.py actually uses external_consensus before claiming it's 'unblocked' — the parameter exists in the function signature but I didn't trace how it flows into the composite score. If the weight is 0.13 (config) but the code path that converts 'BULLISH'/'BEARISH' into a numeric score is broken, we fixed nothing visible. 2) Should have run a live integration test (start server, hit /api/signal, check that 'external' component != 0.0) rather than relying purely on unit tests of the validator. 3) The sector.py fix fetches fred_data inside _analyze_sectors which adds a network call — should have checked if fetcher.fetch_fred_data() is cached or if this doubles the FRED API load on sector requests. 4) drift_detector.py and return_model.py are still dead code — flagged but not wired. Wiring them would have been higher value than writing 53 tests for one service.
next_steps: Verify signal_engine.py actually converts external_consensus string to numeric score (trace the code path)
Run live integration test: GET /api/signal and check components.external != 0.0
Wire drift_detector.py to a /api/drift or /api/data-quality endpoint
Wire return_model.py to stock analysis or portfolio projection
Narrow remaining broad excepts in market.py _compute_market_status (3 blocks)
Add integration test that mocks DataFetcher and verifies end-to-end signal computation with external validator
